IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Friday, July 10, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot. Meade Electric's got 30 Inside JW slots on the Meta Data Center in Lebanon at **$53.80/hr plus 10% above scale** for July 15 & 20—that's real money for a short sprint. Same outfit's pushing 15 CE Level 2+ roles at the same bump. Ermco's Google Mooresville gig is outside new construction but extensive background required. Industrial Electric's IU Hospital work (5-10s, Saturdays included) runs cleaner if you're AC-fluent. 41 calls, 204 hands on the board—book depth here is moderate.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** is the sleeper. Four contractors—Gerdes, Pieper, Staff, Kelso Burnett—are all stacked on the Microsoft Mount Pleasant project at **$62.51/hr scale**. That's top-tier pay for the Midwest. Long sleeves, safety toes, and WI reciprocal license required. Start dates already live (late June). Just 76 hands, 11 calls—this board's tight.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is chasing Polaris Forge. Parsons Electric's running 50 + 28 JW calls at **$44.80/hr base with $100/day nontaxed per diem** (100+ mile radius) and **$100–$150 daily incentives Mon–Sat**. Real per diem, real incentives. Hunt Electric's Grand Forks Air Base adds 10 more at the same rate. 6–10s, hard schedule, but the math works: 130 hands chasing 26 calls means boards are moving.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** has Lake Mariner Data Center locked down. O'Connell and Ferguson are both running 35+ and 27 Inside JW slots at **$48.20/hr, 7/10s schedule, per diem available**. Amazon work (BCU) rounds it out at scale. 103 hands, 9 calls—boards clearing.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** keeps Port Washington data center primed. Gurtz, MJ, and Uihlein all offer **JW wage plus $10/hr** (valid WI license) across 50–58 hour weeks. Pieper's healthcare gig adds variety. 36 calls, 172 hands.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** stays solid. Inglett & Stubbs' QTS and Wellstar hospital work anchor 66 slots at **$40–$42.18/hr**. Miller's cable splicer role (QTS Fayette) pays **$40.90** but needs three years documented splice time. 38 calls, 220 hands.

**The trend:** Data centers are the magnet. Every major local is running DC work, and the pay bump (over-scale premiums, nontaxed per diem, incentives) tells you contractors are desperate to fill long-term projects. If you're licensed and willing to work 5–6 day weeks through 2026, the work is there.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Friday, July 10, 2026**

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is running hot with 26 calls and ~130 hands needed. Parsons Electric's got 78 slots on the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood pulling **$44.80/hr** plus **$100/day per diem** on 6-10's—ND license reciprocated in 30 days if you're clean. Hunt Electric's offering **$44.80** at Grand Forks Air Base with Friday OT kicks on 4-10's. The real money's Apex Line Contractors' 6 journeyman slots at full scale **$58.50** and **$100/day incentive**, running 6-12's. Book depth's real here.

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** has 46 calls but only ~60 hands on the board—tightest ratio in this snapshot. Golden Valley's paying **$30.86/hr** for 8-week inventory work in Healy; Morley Electric's got 2 inside JW slots at Delta Junction with housing or per diem covered. New Horizons' wage scale swings wild (**$24.18–$55.06**) depending on experience across statewide locations.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** is a META play. VECA Electric's got 10 slots (day and swing) at **$65.33/hr** under the new MOU effective April '26—META's backing the scale. Lea Electric's Micron PLA work pays **$45.33** but adds **$20/hr travel pay** and **$170/day per diem** x 7 days if eligible. Quality Electric's St. Luke's tower work is **$55.33** on shop calls, 5-8's.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** shows 44 hands for 3 calls—nearly clear. ArchKey's Google Data Center job (West Memphis) needs 38 inside JW's at **$36.65/hr** plus **$125/day incentive**, 4-10's and 2-8's mixed. Gephart's running 6-10's with double-time OT; R&W's on 5-12's with Saturday OT.

**LU-194 Shreveport, LA** is a FiberBond long call. Caddo and EMS both staffing 20 inside JW's each on 5-10's plus every other Saturday, adding 10% premiums.

Data-center infrastructure and fiber builds are pulling hands nationwide—this market's riding the AI buildout wave hard.
